title: Martha Graham
extract: Martha Graham was an American modern dancer, teacher and choreographer responsible for creating the Graham technique, the most widely taught method of contemporary dance to this day. She is widely considered one of the most important figures in the history of dance, having choreographed over 180 works throughout her seven-decade career. Graham received the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 1976. Time magazine recognized Graham as the dancer of the century, and she is credited with bringing new expressive freedom and symbolism to concert dance.
